none: Tulsa Urban Renewal Authority river parks project permit : environmental impact statement.

The Tulsa Urban Renewal Authority proposed a project to develop an urban park along the Arkansas River in Tulsa, Oklahoma. The plan included bank stabilization and re-alignment, a marina, a lighted fountain, and overlooks. A controversial low water weir was initially part of the project but was withdrawn due to concerns over water quality and fish migration. The project aimed to enhance neglected areas but raised environmental concerns, including potential impacts on wildlife habitats and water quality.
Tulsa Urban Renewal Authority river parks project permit : environmental impact statement.

The Tulsa Urban Renewal Authority proposed a project to develop an urban park along the Arkansas River in Tulsa, Oklahoma. The plan included bank stabilization and re-alignment, a marina, a lighted fountain, and overlooks. A controversial low water weir was initially part of the project but was withdrawn due to concerns over water quality and fish migration. The project aimed to enhance neglected areas but raised environmental concerns, including potential impacts on wildlife habitats and water quality.

The Tulsa Urban Renewal Authority proposed the river parks project to revitalize neglected areas along the Arkansas River and provide recreational opportunities for residents. However, the project faced public pushback, particularly regarding the low water weir, which was ultimately withdrawn due to concerns about its impact on water quality and fish migration. Despite these challenges, the project aimed to enhance the urban landscape and improve community access to the river.
United States. Army. Corps of Engineers
United States. Army. Corps of Engineers

"The bank realignment project between the 11th Street Bridge and the 21st Street Bridge will improve the unsightly conditions existing on the westbank of the Arkansas River." — Indian Nations Council of Governments

"While we fundamentally support the development of the banks of the River for an urban park, we feel it is in the best interest of both the River and the Tulsa recreational community to remove the low water weir from the permit until such time as adequate information can be gathered to properly evaluate the long-term impact such a structure will have on the Arkansas River." — Tulsa City-County Health Department
